sql_array: CREATE TABLE tModels (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), description TEXT, version VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO tModels (id, name, description, version) VALUES (1, 'Model1', 'Description1', 'v1.0');
INSERT INTO tModels (id, name, description, version) VALUES (2, 'Model2', 'Description2', 'v2.0');
INSERT INTO tModels (id, name, description, version) VALUES (3, 'Model3', 'Description3', 'v3.0');
SELECT uddi_delete_tModel(name || ' - ' || description) FROM tModels WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM tModels WHERE version = 'v1.0') OR name IS NULL;
SELECT id, name || ' ' || COALESCE(version, 'no version') AS version_info FROM tModels GROUP BY id, name ORDER BY name;
SELECT uddi_delete_tModel(name || ' (Version: ' || version || ')') FROM tModels HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 GROUP BY version;
SELECT uddi_delete_tModel(CONCAT(name, ' with ID: ', CAST(id AS VARCHAR(10)))) FROM tModels WHERE id < (SELECT MAX(id) FROM tModels) AND description IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE tModelLogs (log_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, model_id INT REFERENCES tModels(id), log_message TEXT, log_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO tModelLogs (model_id, log_message) VALUES (1, 'Deleted model successfully');
SELECT uddi_delete_tModel(name || ' - ' || version) FROM tModels WHERE version IS NOT NULL AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tModelLogs WHERE model_id = tModels.id);
